title: chore(project-manager): short-circuit DRAFT-QUEST too
author: Circuit-Overtime
pr_body_excerpt:
## Changes Made

- `project-manager.py` now early-returns on `DRAFT-QUEST` the same way it already does for `POLLEN-QUEST`. Without this, a fresh issue opened with `DRAFT-QUEST` gets classified by the AI and routed into Dev/Support in parallel with the Quest project's Auto-add rule picking it up — issue ends up in two projects.
- DRAFT-QUEST issues are quest candidates waiting for a reward to be sized. They belong only on the Quest board.

## Checks

- [ ] Update the Quest project's "Auto-add to project" filter in Settings → Workflows to match `label:POLLEN-QUEST,DRAFT-QUEST` (comma = OR), so DRAFT-QUEST issues actually land there. This PR is a no-op until that filter update is in.
- [ ] File a new issue labeled `DRAFT-QUEST` and confirm it lands only in the Quest project (not Dev/Support).
- [ ] No regression for `POLLEN-QUEST` issues — same code path, same behavior.


> Nice name for the PR right? haha

gist.headline: Sorting the Quest Board
gist.blurb: Taught our project manager bot to properly sort draft quests so they don't get lost in the dev board shuffle.
gist.summary: Updated the GitHub project manager script to early-return on DRAFT-QUEST labels, preventing draft issues from being incorrectly routed to the Dev/Support board.
gist.impact: Draft quests will now exclusively land on the Quest board for reward sizing instead of duplicating across multiple project boards.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot acting as a project manager, sorting quest scrolls or tickets into specific bins. Represents the GitHub automation script correctly routing DRAFT-QUEST issues.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny manager's visor, sorting glowing quest scrolls into a wooden bin labeled 'QUESTS'. A retro computer monitor in the background displays a clean Kanban board. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
